sourceGroups.name: Standards & Official Documentation
sourceGroups.summary: SQL standards and official database documentation.

sourceGroups.name: Books & Deep References
sourceGroups.summary: In-depth technical books on SQL, databases, and data systems.

sourceGroups.name: Practitioner Resources
sourceGroups.summary: Tutorials, blogs, and community resources for SQL practitioners.

sources.title: ISO/IEC 9075 SQL Standard
sources.author: ISO/IEC
sources.summary: The international standard for SQL, defining the language's syntax, semantics, and conformance requirements.
sources.relevance: Authoritative reference for SQL syntax and behavior across all levels.

sources.title: PostgreSQL Documentation
sources.author: PostgreSQL Global Development Group
sources.summary: Comprehensive documentation for PostgreSQL, covering SQL syntax, administration, performance tuning, and internals.
sources.relevance: Primary dialect reference used throughout the framework examples.

sources.title: Use The Index, Luke!
sources.author: Markus Winand
sources.summary: A guide to database performance for developers, focused on SQL indexing and execution plan analysis.
sources.relevance: Core reference for Levels 3-4 performance optimization and index strategy.

sources.title: Database Internals
sources.author: Alex Petrov
sources.summary: A deep dive into database storage engines, distributed systems, and internal algorithms.
sources.relevance: Key reference for Level 4-5 concepts around storage, replication, and distributed architectures.

sources.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
sources.author: Martin Kleppmann
sources.summary: Comprehensive guide to the principles behind reliable, scalable, and maintainable data systems.
sources.relevance: Foundation for Level 5 distributed data architecture and consistency trade-offs.

sources.title: High Performance MySQL
sources.author: Silvia Botros, Jeremy Tinley
sources.summary: Practical guide to MySQL optimization, covering schema design, indexing, query optimization, and replication.
sources.relevance: MySQL-specific depth for Levels 3-4 performance and schema design.

sources.title: SQL Style Guide
sources.author: Simon Holywell
sources.summary: A consistent style guide for writing clean, readable SQL based on Joe Celko's conventions.
sources.relevance: Reference for Collaboration & Standards dimension across all levels.

sources.title: Modern SQL
sources.author: Markus Winand
sources.summary: Coverage of modern SQL features including window functions, CTEs, lateral joins, and other post-SQL:1999 additions.
sources.relevance: Key resource for Level 2-3 analytical techniques and modern query patterns.
